title: phanera bracteata subsp. bracteata
intro: Phanera bracteata subsp. bracteata, also known as the velvet flower or velvet pod tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant's roots and leaves have been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots are often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
